description: Registry of valid regulatory citations Shadow persona rationales may reference. LLM must pick from this list; generated citations that do not match a registry entry receive REWORK not APPROVE. Load-bearing per red-team A1 (hallucinated section numbers) and A3 (stale citations after amendment). Primary-source URLs given for every entry so bank counsel can verify verbatim. Entries marked verbatim_verified false are pending Loredana review before external procurement use.

12CFR1002.9(b)(2).verbatim_snippet: The statement of reasons for adverse action required by paragraph (a)(2)(i) of this section must be specific and indicate the principal reason(s) for the adverse action. Statements that the adverse action was based on the creditor's internal standards or policies or that the applicant, joint applicant, or similar party failed to achieve a qualifying score on the creditor's credit scoring system are insufficient.

12CFR1002.6(b).verbatim_snippet: A creditor may not consider in its evaluation of creditworthiness any information that it is barred by § 1002.5 from obtaining or from using for any purpose other than to conduct a self-test under § 1002.15.

15USC1691(a).verbatim_snippet: It shall be unlawful for any creditor to discriminate against any applicant, with respect to any aspect of a credit transaction (1) on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, sex or marital status, or age (provided the applicant has the capacity to contract); (2) because all or part of the applicant's income derives from any public assistance program.

CFPB-Circular-2023-03.title: CFPB
CFPB-Circular-2023-03.verbatim_snippet: Creditors may not rely on the checklist of reasons provided in sample forms in the appendix to Regulation B, or elsewhere, if those reasons do not specifically and accurately indicate the principal reason(s) for the adverse action.

CFPB-Circular-2022-03.title: CFPB
CFPB-Circular-2022-03.verbatim_snippet: Federal consumer financial laws that apply to adverse action notifications apply equally when the credit decision is based on complex algorithms or machine learning models. Creditors must be able to identify and disclose the specific principal reasons for taking an adverse action.
CFPB-Circular-2022-03.pending_review_note: Loredana verification pending: verbatim quote paraphrased from Circular 2022-03 summary. Verify against source_url before shipping to bank counsel.

CFPB-Bulletin-2024-09.title: CFPB
CFPB-Bulletin-2024-09.verbatim_snippet: Adverse action notifications tied to complex AI decision-making models must be model-traceable to the specific factor that materially influenced the decision, and avoid generic template phrases that fail the specificity requirement of 12 CFR 1002.9(b)(2).
CFPB-Bulletin-2024-09.pending_review_note: Loredana verification pending: Circular 2026-03 verbatim to be confirmed. Shadow tests reference this Bulletin extensively; verify official CFPB text and update snippet before external procurement use.

SR-26-2.title: Fed SR
SR-26-2.subsection: Footnote 3 — generative and agentic AI carve-out
SR-26-2.verbatim_snippet: A banking organization's risk management and governance practices should guide the determination of appropriate governance and controls for any tools, processes, or systems not covered in this guidance.

SR-11-7.title: Fed SR
SR-11-7.verbatim_snippet: Model risk should be managed like other types of risk. Effective challenge, which is the critical analysis by objective, informed parties who can identify model limitations and produce appropriate changes, is the core of model risk management.
SR-11-7.pending_review_note: SR 11-7 rescinded 2026-04-17 per joint Fed/OCC/FDIC action; replaced by SR 26-2. Kept in registry for historical citation validation only; isCitationCurrent() returns false. Do not cite SR 11-7 in new persona rationales.

31CFR1010.410.verbatim_snippet: All records that are required to be retained by this chapter shall be retained for a period of five years and shall be filed or stored in such a way as to be accessible within a reasonable period of time.

31CFR1010.230.verbatim_snippet: Covered financial institutions are required to establish and maintain written procedures that are reasonably designed to identify and verify beneficial owners of legal entity customers and to include such procedures in their anti-money laundering compliance program.
31CFR1010.230.pending_review_note: 31 CFR 1010.230 paraphrased from FinCEN CDD final rule summary; verify verbatim before external procurement use.

FinCEN-NPRM-2026-04-07.title: FinCEN
FinCEN-NPRM-2026-04-07.section: Fundamentally Reform Financial Institution AML Programs
FinCEN-NPRM-2026-04-07.verbatim_snippet: The proposed rule would establish new anti-money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism (AML/CFT) program requirements for covered financial institutions. The proposed rule would require covered financial institutions to establish, implement, and maintain effective, risk-based, and reasonably designed AML/CFT programs.
FinCEN-NPRM-2026-04-07.pending_review_note: Snippet paraphrased from FinCEN press release + Sullivan & Cromwell 2026-04 memo. Comment closed 2026-06-09. Final rule expected late 2026 / early 2027. Consolidates the pre-NPRM four-pillar framework into risk-based AML/CFT program at 31 CFR 1020.210, absorbing 31 CFR 1010.230 CDD as internal policies/procedures/controls. See docs/FINCEN-NPRM-2026-04-07-ALIGNMENT.md.

31CFR1020.210-NPRM-proposed.verbatim_snippet: Each covered financial institution shall establish, implement, and maintain an effective, risk-based, and reasonably designed AML/CFT program. The program shall include, at a minimum: (a) internal policies, procedures, and controls; (b) the designation of a BSA compliance officer; (c) ongoing employee training; (d) independent testing; and shall incorporate customer due diligence procedures as part of internal policies, procedures, and controls.
31CFR1020.210-NPRM-proposed.pending_review_note: 31 CFR 1020.210 as proposed 2026-04-07. Snippet paraphrased from Federal Register text + Sullivan & Cromwell 2026-04 memo. Under Shadow v1.5.25 NPRM_STAGES.NPRM_PROPOSED semantics; verify verbatim against Federal Register final publication before external procurement use.

FFIEC-IS-Booklet-2016.title: FFIEC
FFIEC-IS-Booklet-2016.verbatim_snippet: Financial institutions should ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information and information systems, including the integrity, accessibility, and authenticity of electronic records.
FFIEC-IS-Booklet-2016.pending_review_note: FFIEC IS Booklet snippet paraphrased from the 2016 revision executive summary; verify exact verbatim language before external procurement use. FFIEC does not mandate specific cryptographic primitives (Ed25519 not named); Shadow CCR positioning must remain 'cryptographically enforces integrity above the regulatory floor' not 'required by.'
